chief_complaint: A 19-year-old male presented with acute generalized tonic-clonic seizures.
history: The patient had a history of recurrent urinary tract infections and epilepsy, accompanied by impaired academic performance and an intracardiac repair 8 years prior to presentation for a congenital cyanotic heart disease. He also had dysmorphic features on clinical examination.
exam: Vital signs: normal. Physical examination revealed dysmorphic features.
labs: ECG: normal. Blood tests: severe hypocalcaemia, hypoparathyroidism. Neuroimaging: normal. Genomic microarray analysis (GMA): 22q11.21 deletion.
